label: Placement and Interview Purpose
description: An area where knowledge of computer network and security is useful.

label: Important Topics
description: All the important topics in computer network discussed with example within the session.

label: Important Question Bank
description: A collection of questions discussed to be helpful for university exams.

label: Features of Computer Network (Topic)
description: A first topic learned in the session, describing characteristics of computer networks.

label: Types of Computer Network (Topic)
description: A first topic learned in the session, describing different categories of computer networks.

label: Data Communication and Networking by Fourauzan
description: A recommended book for the Computer Network and Security subject, known for its simple manner and examples.

label: Computer Networks by Andrew S. Tanenbaum
description: A recommended book for the Computer Network and Security subject.

label: Computer Network (Concept)
description: A group of computers connected with each other through wires, optical fibers, or optical links that share data, resources, and messages with each other.

label: Communication Speed (Feature)
description: A feature of computer network that provides fast and efficient communication, exemplified by video/audio calls, email, and WhatsApp messages.

label: File Sharing (Feature)
description: A feature of computer network that allows sharing different files using applications like Google Drive, Shareit, or WhatsApp.

label: Backup (Feature)
description: A feature of computer network where all data is stored centrally on a main server, making it easy to take backups and avoid data loss.

label: Software and Hardware Sharing (Feature)
description: A feature of computer network where applications or software installed on a centralized server can be used by multiple connected computers, requiring installation once and use everywhere in the network.

label: Security (Feature)
description: A feature of computer network providing proper authorization and authentication processes, ensuring users have permission to access particular files and protecting against server security threats.

label: Scalability (Feature)
description: A feature of computer network allowing the addition of new devices to an existing network, extending its reach.

label: Reliability (Feature)
description: A feature of computer network providing an alternative source of communication technique in case of software or hardware failure within a network.

label: Categorization by Size
description: The method by which computer network types are classified.

label: Personal Area Network (PAN)
description: A type of computer network designed to connect devices within a short range, typically around one person, used in home, offices, organization, hospitals as per requirement.

label: Range of PAN
description: The connectivity range of PAN is up to 10 meters, covering an area up to 30 feet.

label: Connectivity in PAN
description: Achieved using Bluetooth, Wi-Fi technology, or wired devices (e.g., USB cable).

label: Wireless Personal Area Network
description: A type of PAN where devices connect through Wi-Fi or Bluetooth.

label: Wired Personal Area Network
description: A type of PAN where devices connect through USB cable.

label: Transmission Speed in PAN
description: Very high.

label: Maintenance of PAN
description: Easy.

label: Cost of PAN
description: Low.

label: PAN Advantages
description: Flexible, provides high efficiency for short network range, easy setup, low cost, does not require frequent installation, is easy and portable, and needs fewer technical skills.

label: PAN Disadvantages
description: Low network coverage area/range, limited to relatively low data rates.

label: Local Area Network (LAN)
description: A type of computer network used only in small, limited areas, like a lab in a college where multiple computers are connected to one network, typically found in schools, colleges, or offices.

label: Connectivity in LAN
description: Achieved using twisted pair or coaxial cable, Wi-Fi or Ethernet technologies.

label: Range of LAN
description: Up to 2 kilometers.

label: Transmission Speed in LAN
description: Very high, around 1000 Mbps.

label: Maintenance of LAN
description: Easy.

label: Security in LAN
description: Provides higher security.

label: Cost of LAN
description: Less cost after the initial setup.

label: LAN Advantages
description: Offers highest speed, supports Ethernet/fiber/wireless transmission medias, and is easy to install with low cost & maintenance.

label: LAN Disadvantages
description: High initial setup cost, restricted size and covers only a limited area, privacy violation as LAN administrators can see personal data files and internet history, and data stored in a single server computer can pose security threats if accessed by unauthorized users.

label: Metropolitan Area Network (MAN)
description: A type of computer network that covers a larger geographical area than PAN and LAN, like a particular city, town, or multiple buildings, used in banks, universities, colleges, military, railways, and airlines within the same city.

label: Connectivity in MAN
description: Interconnects different LANs using technologies like FDDI, CDDI, and ATM.

label: Range of MAN
description: From 5 kilometers to 50 kilometers.

label: Transmission Speed in MAN
description: Average, ranging from 50-100 Mbps.

label: Maintenance of MAN
description: Difficult.

label: Cost of MAN
description: High.

label: MAN Advantages
description: High-speed connectivity, high and strict security level, allows centralized management and control of the network, and makes it easier to monitor and manage network resources and security.

label: MAN Disadvantages
description: Architecture is quite complicated, is highly expensive due to the high cost to set up fiber optics, and has a data transfer rate that is low when compared to LANs.

label: Wide Area Network (WAN)
description: A type of computer network that extends over a large geographical area such as states or countries.

label: Connectivity in WAN
description: Connected through telephonic lines, fiber optic cables, or satellite links.

label: Range of WAN
description: Above 50 kilometers.

label: Maintenance of WAN
description: Very high.

label: Cost of WAN
description: Very high.

label: The Internet
description: One of the biggest WANs in the world.

label: WAN Advantages
description: Covers large geographical area, transmits data quickly, provides centralized data (no need to buy emails, files, or backup servers), is helpful for different social media messaging to develop global business, shares different software & hardware resources, and provides high bandwidth which increases the data transfer rate.

label: WAN Disadvantages
description: More security issues (needs Firewall & antivirus software), high installation cost, and troubleshooting a particular problem is difficult due to the connection of lots of LAN and MAN networks.

label: University Exam
description: Exams for which the important question bank will be helpful.

label: Global Business
description: A benefit enabled by wide area network through social media messaging.

label: Computer Networks
description: The subject area covered by the course.

label: Classification of Computer Networks
description: The categorization method for computer networks discussed in the session.

label: Wired LAN
description: A type of Local Area Network that uses cables.

label: Wireless LAN
description: A type of Local Area Network that is wireless.

label: Ethernet
description: The well known and most popular technology in wired LAN, used to connect two or more nodes.

label: Ethernet Straight Through Cable
description: A cable used to connect devices of different kinds.

label: Ethernet Crossover Cable
description: A cable used to connect a computer with another computer directly.

label: Hub
description: An intermediary device in a wired local area network.

label: Switch
description: An intermediary device in a wired local area network.

label: Wi-Fi
description: An example technology for a wireless LAN.

label: Router
description: A device needed to connect two local area networks.

label: Bridge
description: A device needed to connect two local area networks.

label: Telecommunications Network
description: Any communications at a distance.

label: Internet
description: A wide wide area network where many local area networks, metropolitan area networks, and wide area networks across the world are connected to each other.

label: New Trends in Computer Networks
description: Emerging trends in the computer networking field.

label: Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)
description: A scenario where employees are encouraged by their employers to bring their own device and connect to the office network.

label: Online Collaboration
description: The ability for participants to collaborate with others from wherever they are with the help of computer networks.

label: Cloud Computing
description: The on-demand availability of computer resources, especially data storage and computing power, without direct active management by the user.

label: Google Drive
description: An example of cloud computing where one can store files and folders.
